methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from oil spill sites and preparing them for laboratory analysis.

steps.description: Four oil spill sites within the study area were selected for the study. All sampling points were geo-referenced.
steps.searchable_summary: select and geo-reference oil spill sites for sediment sampling

steps.description: At each of the identified sampling sites, sediment samples were carried out using a stainless steel Eckman grab to a depth of about 10cm.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using Eckman grab

steps.description: The sediment samples were immediately transferred in plastic containers and frozen at -4°C for transport to Devine Concept Laboratory for samples preparation and analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store and transport sediment samples at -4°C

steps.description: At the laboratory, coarse materials (>2mm) like pebbles and plant debris were removed from the sediment samples.
steps.searchable_summary: remove coarse materials from sediment samples

methods.description: Measurement of various parameters directly at the sampling sites using a multiparameter water quality monitor.

steps.description: pH, temperature, redox potential (Eh), and electrical conductivity (EC) were measured in-situ using a multiparameter water quality monitor (Orion Model, 1260).
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH, temperature, Eh, and EC in-situ

steps.description: At the determination of any of the parameter, the equipment was properly checked and calibrated before and after use.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate equipment before and after parameter measurement

methods.method_name: Particle Size Distribution and Metal Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of particle size distribution and heavy metal content in sediment samples.

steps.description: Samples for metal analysis were sieved at 200µm and stored in a dry atmosphere before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve samples at 200µm and store in dry atmosphere

steps.description: The particle size distribution (PSD) was determined using the hydrometer method followed by sieving the sand fraction (>63µm) from silt (<63 and 2µm) and clay (<2µm).
steps.searchable_summary: determine PSD using hydrometer and sieving

steps.description: Levels of heavy metals in the sediment samples were measured using ATI Unicom Atomic Absorption Spectrophotometer, Model 939.
steps.searchable_summary: measure heavy metal levels using atomic absorption spectrophotometer
